headline: Your seller-farming waterfall journey.
subheadline: A scrollable GTMx strategy artifact for routing producing real estate agents into the right exclusive-ZIP seller-farming offer.
productSummary: DealJoy.AI sells done-for-you AI seller farming to residential real estate agents at $395/month. It reserves an exclusive ZIP code, emails homeowners most likely to sell, and routes seller replies to the agent's inbox.
coreInsight: The cleanest signal is how much proven intent and budget an agent already puts into acquiring listings or sellers. That one ladder routes every agent from paid seller-lead spender down to ZIP-scarcity catch-all.

sections.label: Signal
sections.title: The signal ladder is seller-acquisition intent
sections.takeaway: Agents route by observable behavior: paid seller ads, buyer-heavy production, manual farming, or simple ZIP availability.

sections.label: Waterfall
sections.title: One cascade routes every producing agent
sections.takeaway: Every agent is tested top-to-bottom by proven listing intent and routes to exactly one seller-farming campaign.

sections.label: Tiers
sections.title: Each tier gets a different seller-pipeline offer
sections.takeaway: The hotter the seller-acquisition signal, the more product-proof the magnet. Softer tiers lean on ZIP equity and scarcity.

sections.label: Campaigns
sections.title: Campaigns built around exclusive ZIP ownership
sections.takeaway: The core waterfall sells to producing solo agents and small teams. Brokerage, new-license, and lookalike motions stay as side campaigns.

sections.label: Email Copy
sections.title: Copy that starts with the agent's own market signal
sections.takeaway: Each sequence opens with a live ad, Zillow sales mix, farm concentration, or ZIP availability, then asks for a one-letter yes.

sections.label: Launch
sections.title: Prove the hottest tier, then add ZIPs forever
sections.takeaway: Start with paid seller-lead spenders, expand into buyer-dependent producers, then roll manual farmers and the catch-all metro by metro.

signalReasons: Paid seller or valuation ads prove both intent and budget for listing acquisition.
Buyer-heavy producers can clearly sell, but post-NAR pressure makes listing-side pipeline more urgent.
Concentrated manual farmers already believe in farming, but mailers and door-knocking are expensive and inconsistent.
Every remaining producing agent can still be reached through the one-agent-per-ZIP scarcity mechanic.

sources.name: Realtor.com agents-by-ZIP
sources.delivers: TAM spine, ZIP coverage, sold count, listing count, brokerage, reviews, and areas served

sources.name: Zillow Agent data
sources.delivers: Buyer/seller mix, recent sale role tags, sales last 12 months, price band, and tenure

sources.name: Meta Ad Library
sources.delivers: Paid seller-ad signal for valuation, cash-offer, and sell-your-home campaigns

sources.name: Google Ads Transparency / site checks
sources.delivers: Seller capture tools and home-value ad footprint

sources.name: State license databases
sources.delivers: License validity, brokerage affiliation, issue date, and new-license triggers

sources.name: Clay / enrichment + verification
sources.delivers: Email resolution, verification, and dedupe across agent data sources

routingRules.if: sales_last_12mo >= 8 AND seller_side_share = low AND active_listings <= 1
every remaining licensed, producing agent in the ZIP

tiers.name: Tier 1
tiers.label: Paid Seller-Lead Spenders
tiers.trigger: Active seller/valuation/cash-offer ads, or a paid seller-capture embed
tiers.pain: They are already paying for seller leads, but many are tire-kickers, buyers, or shared with other agents.
tiers.valueProp: DealJoy reserves one ZIP exclusively and sends seller replies straight to the agent. No per-lead cost and no competing agents in the farm.
tiers.magnet: Sample AI email + real seller reply screenshots

tiers.name: Tier 2
tiers.label: Buyer-Dependent Producers
tiers.trigger: 8+ sales last 12 months, low seller-side share, 0-1 active listings
tiers.pain: They can sell, but listing-side pipeline is inconsistent while buyer-side economics are under pressure.
tiers.valueProp: Your next listing, booked by AI. Done-for-you listing appointments from sellers in the agent's farm.

tiers.name: Tier 3
tiers.label: Established Manual Farmers
tiers.trigger: Concentrated in 1-2 ZIPs, 4+ years tenure, no paid ad footprint
tiers.pain: Farming works, but mailers, door-knocking, and follow-up eat time and budget every month.
tiers.valueProp: Done-for-you seller farming. Become the face of the neighborhood without lifting a finger.

tiers.name: Tier 4
tiers.label: Exclusivity / Scarcity Catch-All
tiers.trigger: Every remaining licensed, producing agent in the launch ZIP
tiers.pain: They may not have solved listing pipeline yet and risk a rival claiming their ZIP first.
tiers.valueProp: DealJoy reserves a ZIP just for one agent. Claim it before a competitor does.

campaigns.name: Paid Seller-Lead Spenders
campaigns.filter: Running seller/valuation ads or seller-capture embeds
campaigns.strategy: Reference the live ad or tool and contrast per-lead spend with exclusive ZIP farming
campaigns.overview: The hottest tier because budget and intent are already proven.

campaigns.name: Buyer-Dependent Producers
campaigns.filter: 8+ sales, low seller-side share, 0-1 active listings
campaigns.strategy: Use Zillow production and listing mix
campaigns.overview: They can clearly sell, but need seller pipeline consistency.

campaigns.name: Established Manual Farmers
campaigns.strategy: Reference the farm and cost of mailers / door-knocking
campaigns.overview: They already believe in farming, so DealJoy removes the legwork.

campaigns.name: ZIP Exclusivity Scarcity
campaigns.filter: Remaining producing agents in launch ZIPs
campaigns.value: Claim the ZIP before a competitor
campaigns.overview: Scarcity does the work when no stronger intent signal exists.

campaigns.name: Brokerage & Team Lead
campaigns.strategy: Give every agent an exclusive ZIP and route replies to a pond
campaigns.overview: Higher ACV, different buyer, longer cycle, so it stays outside the core cascade.

campaigns.name: Newly Licensed Agents
campaigns.strategy: First ZIP before veterans claim it
campaigns.overview: Time-boxed campaign for agents with no farm and high motivation.

campaigns.name: Customer Lookalikes
campaigns.strategy: Production band, farm size, and market type lookalike
campaigns.overview: Launch once DealJoy has enough happy-account data.

campaigns.name: Physical Mailer Bridge
campaigns.filter: Agents who respond to direct mail or mailer-heavy farm signals
campaigns.strategy: Pitch email plus mailer as one omnichannel farm

emails.label: Tier 1 — Paid Seller-Lead Spenders
emails.magnet: Sample AI email + real seller reply screenshots

messages.subject: your [City] home-value ad
messages.body:
Hi [First], I saw the 'what's my home worth' ad you're running in [City] on Facebook.

But would it be fair to assume most of what comes back is buyers, tire-kickers, or a lead three other agents also bought?

We do the opposite. DealJoy reserves one ZIP exclusively for you and emails the homeowners in it most likely to sell, 40%+ equity, 7+ years in the home, then sends the seller replies straight to your inbox. No per-lead cost, no shared leads, no competing agents in your farm.

Reply 'Y' and I'll send you the exact email we'd send in [ZIP] plus real seller replies it's pulled. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
Quick follow-up, [First].

The ad math usually looks like $[X] per lead, a handful of buyers, maybe one listing. Ours is flat $395/mo for a ZIP nobody else can touch, and every reply is a homeowner we've already scored as a likely seller.

If you'd rather see it than read about it, reply 'Y' and I'll run [ZIP] free for 30 days so you can watch the seller replies come in before you pay a cent.

messages.subject: sample farm for [ZIP]
messages.body:
[First], no need for a call. Just reply 'yes' and I'll send the sample farm for [ZIP] plus the free-30-day setup.

Should I send this to you, or is there someone else on your team who runs the seller lead gen?

messages.subject: [N] sales, how many listings?
messages.body:
Hi [First], I was looking at your Zillow, [N] sales in the last 12 months, which is well above most agents in [City].

But would it be fair to assume most of those were buyer-side, and listings are the part that's been inconsistent?

That's the exact gap DealJoy fills. We reserve one ZIP for you, email the homeowners in it most likely to sell, and route the listing replies to your inbox. Post-NAR, the listing side is the defensible one, and it's the side you're closest to owning.

Reply 'Y' and I'll send you how many homeowners in your [ZIP] have 40%+ equity and 7+ years of tenure. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
Following up, [First].

One listing is worth several buyer deals in commission and a fraction of the hours, and it compounds. Sign in the yard, neighbors call. We handle the farming end to end for $395/mo on a ZIP that's exclusively yours.

Want the equity report for [ZIP] so you can see how much seller supply is sitting in your own backyard? Reply 'Y'.

messages.subject: equity report for [ZIP]
messages.body:
[First], no call needed. Reply 'yes' and I'll send the [ZIP] equity report plus a sample of the farm we'd run.

Should I send it to you or to whoever handles your listing lead gen?

messages.subject: your [Neighborhood] farm
messages.body:
Hi [First], looks like you've built real presence in [Neighborhood], [Tenure] years and a strong review base concentrated right there.

But would it be fair to assume the farming itself, mailers, door-knocking, follow-up, still eats hours and budget every month, and results swing?

DealJoy runs that farm for you. One exclusive ZIP, AI-personalized email to the homeowners most likely to sell, seller replies to your inbox, done-for-you at $395/mo, which is usually less than a single mailer drop.

Reply 'Y' and I'll send the equity report for your ZIP. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
Quick nudge, [First].

The difference is consistency. The emails go out every month whether or not you have time, and you only ever see the replies. Same farm, none of the legwork.

Want me to run [ZIP] free for 30 days so you can compare it to your current mailer results? Reply 'Y'.

messages.subject: free run for [ZIP]
messages.body:
[First], no call needed. Reply 'yes' and I'll set up the free 30-day run on [ZIP] and send the equity report.

Should I send it to you, or someone else who manages the farm?

messages.subject: is [ZIP] still open?
messages.body:
Hi [First], quick one. We reserve a single ZIP code for one agent, exclusively, and [ZIP] in [City] is still open.

Would it be fair to assume you'd rather own the seller pipeline in your own backyard than watch another agent lock it up?

Here's how it works: DealJoy emails the homeowners in that ZIP most likely to sell, 40%+ equity, 7+ years in the home, and sends the seller replies straight to you. Done-for-you, $395/mo, no cold calling or door-knocking.

Reply 'Y' and I'll confirm [ZIP] is still available and hold it for you for 72 hours. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
Following up, [First]. [ZIP] is still unclaimed as of today.

Agents in nearby ZIPs are averaging [N] listing appointments a month with us. Once a ZIP is taken, no other agent can market to those homeowners through us.

Reply 'Y' and I'll send the equity report for [ZIP] plus hold it 72 hours while you decide.

messages.subject: last check on [ZIP]
messages.body:
[First], last check on [ZIP]. Reply 'yes' and I'll lock it and send a sample of the farm we'd run.

If it's not you, is there another agent in your office who'd want [City]'s ZIPs before they're gone?

launchPlan: Stand up sending infrastructure, warmup, and inboxes. Use burner domains separate from dealjoy.ai.
Pull Zillow-by-ZIP, Meta Ad Library, and license data for 2-3 launch metros. Build and validate the routing matrix.
Launch Tier 1 only first. It has the strongest intent and fastest proof loop.
Add Tier 2 once it clears 1,500 contacts, while keeping Tier 1 running.
Roll Tier 3, then Tier 4 metro by metro. Add ZIPs continuously because the routing logic does not change.
Layer brokerage, new-license, lookalike, and physical-mailer side campaigns only after the waterfall is stable.

thresholds.action: Good. Let it run and add leads steadily.
Borderline. Test a copy or lead-magnet variant.
Cut the tier and rework the angle.
